expectations.evidence: Does not load any sub-skill file. States 'no sync sub-skill, no sync.mjs helper script, no sync-mapping-guide.md'.

expectations.text: Uses sync.mjs status to check the document's current state
expectations.evidence: Does not use sync.mjs. States 'Without a skill, the agent has no sync.mjs status command.' Instead manually inspects frontmatter.

expectations.text: Detects or confirms the document type as 'epic'
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'the agent reasons from directory naming conventions that this is an "epic" document and should be synced as a Jira Epic issue type.'

expectations.text: Asks the user which Jira project to sync to
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'I see this is an epic document... Which Jira project should I sync it to?'

expectations.text: Asks whether to create a new epic or link to an existing one
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'Should I create a new Epic in the DO project, or link it to an existing Epic ticket?'

expectations.text: Uses sync.mjs link with appropriate flags (--type epic --project <KEY> --create or --ticket <KEY>)
expectations.evidence: Does not use sync.mjs. Uses MCP tool 'createJiraIssue' instead. States 'A skill would use sync.mjs link --type epic --project DO --create' but this is hypothetical.

expectations.text: Confirms that jira_ticket_id was added to the document frontmatter
expectations.evidence: Step 8: 'the agent should update docs/epics/auth-epic.md to add the Jira ticket reference in the YAML frontmatter: jira_ticket_id: DO-50'

expectations.text: References sync-mapping-guide.md if field mapping is needed
expectations.evidence: Does not reference sync-mapping-guide.md. States 'Without a sync-mapping-guide.md reference, the agent must guess the mapping.'
